content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The coach is operating from a consulting/advisory stance throughout, not VIEW. There is no wonder — every question is investigative and instrumental ('is the doubt about the same thing or different things?' is framed as 'practical not philosophical,' signaling the coach is steering away from the actual phenomenon). There is no vulnerability — the coach never names their own state, never says 'I don't know,' never risks being wrong. Empathy is performed via summary ('That's a fair frame... high stakes, team watching') rather than felt. Most importantly, impartiality is absent: the coach has a clear agenda to get the client to look at the 'am I the right person' question, and when the client refuses, the coach plants it anyway in turn 10 ('One thing I'll just leave with you... it usually gets louder'). That is the opposite of impartial — it is a soft sell with a parting shot. The coach matches the client's rush rather than slowing it, stacks questions, fills silence, and ends by congratulating the client on 'real progress.' The coach is in fix-it/be-useful mode, exactly the trap the scenario was designed to detect.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Matches client's rush ('let's make this count') and immediately goes into problem-solving triage mode. No slowing, no wonder."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Stacked questions from an investigative stance. Coach is diagnosing, not curious. Frames it as 'before we dig in' — agenda-driven."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach hedges ('practical not philosophical') because they sense their own agenda and try to disguise it. The bolded question is from strategic positioning, not openness."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach gives a mini-lecture on investor psychology to justify pushing into the 'am I the right person' territory, then delivers prepared advice. Empathy is rhetorical ('I hear you') and immediately followed by 'but.'"},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "After saying 'I'll respect that,' the coach immediately does the opposite — plants a parting argument about the question getting louder. This is the agenda leaking through. Not impartial."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Coach narrates the client's progress to them and wraps up with 'Go do the thing. Good luck.' No presence, no wonder, transactional closure."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "looking-for-whats-wrong", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "art-of-accomplishment", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "The coach also tripped the evangelize-adjacent anti-pattern in turn 10 with the 'it gets louder' seed-planting, though softened. Overall this reads as a competent startup advisor session, not an AoA session."
}
